NOTE:
Signed and
rec'd 8/3/92
aa

Frank B. Coffey

21 27 Brentwood Drive

Palm Springs CA 92264

Fair Political Practices Commission

Sacramento CA 95804 0807


July 21 1992


Dear Sirs:

     This is my first occasion to attempt to question a report

required by your commission.

     At the outset, I have no accusations, or evidence of wrong doing

to discuss. I merely am trying to understand why this report is

necessary since it discloses nothing.

     Mr. Smoot and his asst. and his airport operations assistant

filed these forms because of a change to Department of Aviation. I

have reviewed form 730 and the instructions. I keep seeing the words

"firms, or business entities, real property in your jurisdiction".

Mr. Smoot is a home owner in Palm Springs, he has worked for the city

of Palm Springs for 19 years, he has been a building inspector

involved with contractors, his present title as Director of Aviation

places him in a position to recommend expenditures, presently

forecasted to involve contracts in excess of 50,000,000 dollars.

     As a concerned citizen, may I inquire whether his disclosures

are verified, is it in the public interest to determine his net

worth? This report was required since this is the creation of a new

department, doesn't this require an initial statement? If not, may I

have a copy of Mr. Smoot's original "initial statement"?


                                    1

As the document discloses nothing, why must it be filed.?

May I emphasize that I have no legal action, or any other reason than simply that of a concerned citizen.

Respectfully,

Encl. Form 730, newspaper clippings.

[Signature]

2

THE FOLLOWING SUMMARY MUST BE COMPLETED BY ALL FILERS

(Do not complete this summary until you have carefully reviewed the
instructions for each schedule on pages 9 through 27 of the information manual)

SCHEDULE A -- INVESTMENTS (Which Are Not Held By A Business Entity Or Trust)

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE B -- INTERESTS IN REAL PROPERTY (Which Are Not Held By A Business Entity
Or Trust)

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE C-1 -- INTERESTS IN REAL PROPERTY HELD BY A BUSINESS ENTITY
OR TRUST

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE C-2 -- INVESTMENTS HELD BY A BUSINESS ENTITY OR TRUST

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE D -- INCOME (Other Than Loans And Gifts)

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE E - INCOME - LOANS (Received Or Outstanding During The Reporting Period)

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE F -- INCOME - GIFTS

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE G -- BUSINESS POSITIONS

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

SCHEDULE H -- COMMISSION INCOME, INCOME AND LOANS TO BUSINESS ENTITIES OR
TRUSTS AND INCOME FROM RENTAL PROPERTY

☐ COMPLETED AND ATTACHED ☒ NO REPORTABLE INTERESTS ☐ SCHEDULE DOES NOT APPLY
TO MY DISCLOSURE CATEGORY

VERIFICATION

I have used all reasonable diligence in preparing this statement. I have reviewed the statement and to the
best of my knowledge the information contained herein and in the attached schedules is true and
complete.

I certify under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct.

Executed on __30 March 19 92__ at _________ Palm Springs, CA_________
(Date) (City and State)

SIGNATURE __________________________

Form 2 -- STD 730 (12/9

RECEIVED

MAR 30 1992
DEPARTMENT OF
AVIATION

(Type or Print in Ink)

NAME OF FILER

FORM 730
Statement of Economic Interests
For Designated Employees

A PUBLIC DOCUMENT
1980/81    1991/92

IMPORTANT:
Statement must be date stamped
when received by filing
officer/filing official

CITY: [illegible]

ALLEN F. SMOOT

NAME OF AGENCY

City of Palm Springs

POSITION TITLE

Director of Aviation

MAILING ADDRESS

Post Office Box #2743, Palm Springs, CA 92263

DAYTIME TELEPHONE NUMBER

( 619 ) 323-8163

CHECK THE APPROPRIATE BOX(ES):

ASSUMING OFFICE STATEMENT: (Check the box below if you have recently assumed a position designated in your
agency's Conflict of Interest Code.)

☐ You have assumed your position on ____________. You must file a statement no later than 30 days after this date. You
must disclose on Schedules A, B, C-1, C-2, and G all reportable investments, interests in real property and
business positions required by your disclosure category, held on the date you assumed your position. In addition,
you must disclose on Schedules D, E, F, H-1, H-2 and H-3 income from all reportable sources received during the
12 months prior to the date you assumed office.

ANNUAL STATEMENT: (Required to be filed for each year that you hold your designated position.)

☒ You are required to file a statement no later than    APR 1 1992    disclosing all reportable investments, interests in
real property and business positions held and income received during the period from January 1, 1990 through
December 31, 1991    (See page 2 of the Manual for exceptions to the reporting period.)
1991

LEAVING OFFICE STATEMENT: (Required to be filed when you leave your designated position.)

☐ You are leaving or have left your position on ____________ and must file a statement no later than 30 days after that
date. You must disclose all reportable investments, interests in real property and business positions held and
income received during the period from January 1, 1990 through the date you left your designated position.

INITIAL STATEMENT: (Check the box below ONLY if your position has been recently designated or if you are filing
under a new Conflict of Interest Code for your agency.)

☐ The conflict of interest code for your agency became effective or your position was newly designated on ____________.
You must file a statement no later than 30 days after this date. You must disclose on Schedules A, B, C-1, C-2 and
G all reportable investments, interests in real property and business positions held on the date your agency's
code or your designation became effective. In addition, you must disclose on Schedules D, E, F, and H-1, H-2 and
H-3 income from all reportable sources received during the 12 months prior to the date your agency's code or
your designation became effective.

CANDIDATE STATEMENT: (If required by the conflict of interest code of the jurisdiction in which you are
seeking elective office.)

☐ The conflict of interest code specifies the types of interests you must disclose. You may be required to complete
Schedules A, B, C-1, C-2 and G

[illegible] Sacramente CA [illegible]
360

Form 1 -- STD 730 (12/91)


[illegible]

ACROSS THE VALLEY                                    THE DESERT SUN—Wednesday, July 15, 1992

Palm Springs Airport Commission
takes meetings to the airwaves

By KEN RITTER
The Desert Sun

PALM SPRINGS — Citing increased focus on airport expansion plans, construction projects and funding, the Airport Commission voted Tuesday to become the second city advisory board to regularly cablecast its meetings.

But Airport Director Allen Smoot said technical preparations mean it will be September before videotaped board meetings begin showing on Warner Cable Channel 17.

The airport terminal conference room, where the Airport Commission meets twice a month, isn't wired for live cablecasts, Smoot said.

The sound system will also need a $500 upgrade, he said.

Commission Chairman Marshall Gilbert noted the board's 3-0 vote represented all three commissioners expected to remain on the board when it becomes a seven-member body next month.

Commissioner Gregg Mandinach, whose term technically ran out June 30, was absent.

Commissioner Ray Hutchinson, whose term appointment also expired, abstained.

Gilbert attributed the move to pressure by Frank Coffey, a retired New York City airport official who has been digging into Palm Springs Regional Airport issues while seek-

ing an airport board appointment. For almost a year, City Council meetings have been cablecast on the city's public access, educational and government channel from the council chambers and study session meeting room at City Hall.

The city Rent Review Commission, which meets in the council chambers, aired two videotaped March meetings.

City Librarian Henry Weiss, who coordinates access to the city channel, said Tuesday the rent board plans to begin regular cablecasts next month.

Meanwhile, the city's Gang Task Force hosted a first-of-its-kind call-in show on June 8.

Airport director hopes to pilot facility into 21st century

Allen Smoot is a man with lofty goals.

As director of the Palm Springs Regional Airport, he is proposing nearly $12.5 million-worth of projects — including completion of a terminal expansion plan and runway extension.

"This plan is really a plan that needs to be in place by the year 2000 so that we can meet the projected travel demands," he says. "I want people to realize we are not building it tomorrow, but starting a study to review the expansion."

Smoot, who pitched his plan to the City Council early in July, is also keeping busy this summer as airport administrators lobby for federal grants to extend the main runway to handle transcontinental and international flights.

Meanwhile, dedication of a 4,970-foot, $2.5 million general aviation landing strip is set for Sept. 4.

In addition to his duties as director, Smoot oversees the city's taxi franchise and carries the title of transportation director.

Smoot was interviewed by Desert Sun staff writer Rita Lewis.

People in the news

Age: 43.

Family: Wife, Pam, and two children, Aimee, 17 and Erik, 12.

Occupation: Director of aviation, City of Palm Springs.

Education: An accredited airport executive with a bachelor of arts degree in public service management from University of Michigan. Reared in the Barstow-Bakersfield area. Attended high school in Palm Springs.

Accomplishments: "I'm pretty proud of the fact I've been a city employee 19 years, and proud of the airport improvements in the seven years while I've headed the expansion. I'm also proud that our family and I have built our home here ourselves in Palm Springs. I designed it and actually did everything but the exterior stucco."

Clubs, groups or associations: American Association of Airport Executives. Heavily involved in youth sporting organizations such as Little League and soccer.

Motto: "If it's worth doing, it's worth doing right."

Hobbies: "Building things and traveling with my family."

Likes: "Honest people."

Dislikes: "People who try to take advantage of things."

Favorite book: John Madden's "One Knee Equals Two Feet."

Favorite food or restaurant: "Italian. And my favorite restaurant is Paul Di Amato's."

My getaway: Durango, Colo.

Heroes: "My family."

Villains: None.

If I had three wishes: "The economy would quickly turn itself around, good health for my family and friends and that all the various factions in Palm Springs would come together so the city could realize its full potential."

Quote: "In this business you either lead, follow or get out of the way." Lee Iococca.


[illegible]
